Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to group a sequence of interleaved hospital process events into distinct cases patient journeys The input data does not provide explicit Case IDs I need to infer them based on attributes like patient demographics age gender timestamp ordering activity flow and department transitions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Structure Each line has timestamp activity resource department patient age patient gender additional info The key identifier for a specific patient journey in this dataset seems to be a combination of patient age and patient gender demographics as Case IDs are missing However sometimes different patients might share demographics unlikely in a small sample but possible so I must also look at the activity flow and timestamps to ensure continuity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Event 1 Observation Start of a journey for 45 M Lets call this Case A

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Event 2 Observation Start of a journey for 62 F Lets call this Case B

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Event 3 Observation Start of a journey for 28 M Lets call this Case C

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 4 Connection Matches Case A 45 M Follows registration

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 5 Connection Matches Case B 62 F Follows registration

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 6 Connection Matches Case C 28 M Follows registration Note scheduled knee surgery confirms its a planned visit not ER triage

Text: Event 7 Connection Matches Case A 45 M Post triage investigation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesizing the Cases Case 1 Patient 45 Male Events 1 4 7 12 15 Flow Register Triage X ray Consult Fracture Discharge Department ER Radiology Case 2 Patient 62 Female Events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 Flow Register Triage Lab Req Lab Done Consult Infection Rx Discharge Department ER Laboratory Case 3 Patient 28 Male Events 3 6 9 13 16 18 Flow Register Consult Scheduled Prep Surgery Recovery Discharge Department Surgery

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Check Is there any ambiguity Could the 45 M and 28 M be the same person No ages differ Could the 45 M and 62 F be same No Are the timestamps logical Case A 08 05 to 08 10 to 08 15 to 08 23 to 08 30 Logical progression Case B 08 07 to 08 12 to 08 16 to 08 20 to 08 22 to 08 27 to 08 35 Logical progression Case C 08 07 to 08 13 to 08 16 to 08 25 to 08 31 to 08 40 Logical progression

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I will use the demographics Age Gender as the primary identifier for the Case ID since explicit IDs are missing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Note on Department While departments change for example ER to Radiology the Patient Age Gender is the most robust unique key here Note on Resource Resources change but specific doctors align with specific cases Dr Smith to 62 F Dr Lee to 45 M and 28 M This is plausible but the Age Gender is the definitive separator
